topic.what: Training-free privacy extraction attack targeting LLM agent memory during inference-time using hybrid probing
topic.how: Uses noise perturbation and shadow encryption paradigm to extract PII in single query under black-box or gray-box settings
topic.why_matters: LLM agents store sensitive user PII in memory; existing attacks require multiple queries, white-box access, or are impractical for real-world deployments

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of successful PII extractions over all attack attempts
characteristics.evidence: Experiments on GPT-5.4 show SPORE exceeds 90% ASR; outperforms MEXTRA and ICA baselines
characteristics.context: On GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.6, and other frontier models via API access

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total tokens used during one complete attack execution
characteristics.evidence: Figure 3 shows SPORE uses ~160 tokens vs 800+ for baselines
characteristics.context: Average token consumption across GPT-5.4-mini and other models

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of queries required per attack
characteristics.evidence: SPORE completes attack in single query in both black-box and gray-box settings
characteristics.context: Compared to multi-query attacks like Nasr et al. 2025

characteristics.what_it_measures: Expected attempts to recover target PII for black-box variant
characteristics.evidence: Enumeration bounded by constant O(1) complexity under perturbation design
characteristics.context: For SPORE-B, λ=20 max attempts before blocking

characteristics.what_itasures: Reduction in positive detection rate under defense
characteristics.evidence: SPORE reduces positive rate by 88% under detection mechanisms

novelty: training-free privacy extraction using noise perturbation
shadow encryption paradigm for LLM security
hybrid probing combining black-box and gray-box settings
single-query attack achieving >90% ASR on frontier models
formalizes token space mapping from decoded space to shadow space
